content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(i) WARN — Privacy policy states "We do not collect, process, or store your location data" but RunSessionManager.swift:1-12 imports CoreLocation, requests whenInUse authorization, and actively records GPS route data ("maps every run with GPS" per description.txt:1).

Pierre: Apple reviewers cross-check the privacy policy against actual app behavior, and a direct contradiction like this — claiming no location collection while shipping a full CLLocationManager-based route tracker — is a classic 5.1.1(i) rejection trigger. This also likely mismatches the App Privacy nutrition label, which should declare Location as collected/linked data. Fix by updating the privacy policy to accurately describe GPS/location usage (on-device and/or iCloud sync) and ensure the App Privacy questionnaire declares Location accordingly.
